data: Question 11
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What does it mean to "Evaluate and Revise" a design?

[{'answer_text': 'To color the parachute to make it look nice.', 'text': 'To color the parachute to make it look nice.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To throw the parachute away and quit.', 'text': 'To throw the parachute away and quit.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To drop the object without a parachute.', 'text': 'To drop the object without a parachute.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To use the exact same materials again without changes.', 'text': 'To use the exact same materials again without changes.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To look at how well it worked and make improvements to fix problems.', 'text': 'To look at how well it worked and make improvements to fix problems.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To write down the heights of all the buildings in the city.', 'text': 'To write down the heights of all the buildings in the city.', 'weight': 0}]
To color the parachute to make it look nice.
To throw the parachute away and quit.
To drop the object without a parachute.
To use the exact same materials again without changes.
To look at how well it worked and make improvements to fix problems.
To write down the heights of all the buildings in the city.
Question 12
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What type of material is usually best for making the canopy (top part) of a parachute?

[{'answer_text': 'Heavy, thick metal.', 'text': 'Heavy, thick metal.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Hard, solid wood.', 'text': 'Hard, solid wood.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Glass that breaks easily.', 'text': 'Glass that breaks easily.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Sticky, wet mud.', 'text': 'Sticky, wet mud.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Heavy rocks tied together.', 'text': 'Heavy rocks tied together.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Large, lightweight, and flexible material.', 'text': 'Large, lightweight, and flexible material.', 'weight': 100}]
Heavy, thick metal.
Hard, solid wood.
Glass that breaks easily.
Sticky, wet mud.
Heavy rocks tied together.
Large, lightweight, and flexible material.
Question 13
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What is the most likely reason a soft object would crack when dropped from a high building?

[{'answer_text': 'It hits the ground with too much speed and impact force.', 'text': 'It hits the ground with too much speed and impact force.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'It gets too cold while falling through the air.', 'text': 'It gets too cold while falling through the air.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Air resistance squeezes it too hard.', 'text': 'Air resistance squeezes it too hard.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The parachute pulls it apart.', 'text': 'The parachute pulls it apart.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Gravity turns off before it reaches the ground.', 'text': 'Gravity turns off before it reaches the ground.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It bounces too many times on the surface.', 'text': 'It bounces too many times on the surface.', 'weight': 0}]
It hits the ground with too much speed and impact force.
It gets too cold while falling through the air.
Air resistance squeezes it too hard.
The parachute pulls it apart.
Gravity turns off before it reaches the ground.
It bounces too many times on the surface.
Question 14
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What happens to air resistance if you make your parachute wider and larger?

[{'answer_text': 'The air resistance decreases, making it fall faster.', 'text': 'The air resistance decreases, making it fall faster.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The air resistance increases, making it fall slower.', 'text': 'The air resistance increases, making it fall slower.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The air resistance stays exactly the same.', 'text': 'The air resistance stays exactly the same.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The parachute becomes invisible.', 'text': 'The parachute becomes invisible.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Gravity pushes the object upward instead.', 'text': 'Gravity pushes the object upward instead.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object stops moving completely in mid-air.', 'text': 'The object stops moving completely in mid-air.', 'weight': 0}]
The air resistance decreases, making it fall faster.
The air resistance increases, making it fall slower.
The air resistance stays exactly the same.
The parachute becomes invisible.
Gravity pushes the object upward instead.
The object stops moving completely in mid-air.
Question 15
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
Why might an engineer need to safely drop supplies, like food or medicine, from an airplane?

[{'answer_text': 'Because airplanes cannot carry food inside them.', 'text': 'Because airplanes cannot carry food inside them.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To practice aiming at targets on the ground.', 'text': 'To practice aiming at targets on the ground.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To deliver important items to areas that are hard to reach after a disaster.', 'text': 'To deliver important items to areas that are hard to reach after a disaster.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'To see if the food tastes better after falling.', 'text': 'To see if the food tastes better after falling.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Because the supplies are too heavy for trucks.', 'text': 'Because the supplies are too heavy for trucks.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'To test how fast food can travel.', 'text': 'To test how fast food can travel.', 'weight': 0}]
Because airplanes cannot carry food inside them.
To practice aiming at targets on the ground.
To deliver important items to areas that are hard to reach after a disaster.
To see if the food tastes better after falling.
Because the supplies are too heavy for trucks.
To test how fast food can travel.
Question 16
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
Which two forces are competing against each other when a parachute is falling?

[{'answer_text': 'Magnetism and electricity.', 'text': 'Magnetism and electricity.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Wind and fire.', 'text': 'Wind and fire.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Friction and speed.', 'text': 'Friction and speed.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Gravity and air resistance.', 'text': 'Gravity and air resistance.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'Water pressure and heat.', 'text': 'Water pressure and heat.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Sound and light.', 'text': 'Sound and light.', 'weight': 0}]
Magnetism and electricity.
Wind and fire.
Friction and speed.
Gravity and air resistance.
Water pressure and heat.
Sound and light.
Question 17
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
If you test a parachute and it falls too fast, what is the best revision to make?

[{'answer_text': 'Use a heavier object at the bottom.', 'text': 'Use a heavier object at the bottom.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Cut holes in the parachute to let more air out.', 'text': 'Cut holes in the parachute to let more air out.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Take the parachute off entirely.', 'text': 'Take the parachute off entirely.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Drop it from a lower height next time.', 'text': 'Drop it from a lower height next time.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'Increase the size of the parachute to catch more air.', 'text': 'Increase the size of the parachute to catch more air.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'Make the strings much shorter.', 'text': 'Make the strings much shorter.', 'weight': 0}]
Use a heavier object at the bottom.
Cut holes in the parachute to let more air out.
Take the parachute off entirely.
Drop it from a lower height next time.
Increase the size of the parachute to catch more air.
Make the strings much shorter.
Question 18
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
During a 10-trial experiment, one of your drop times is completely different from the other nine (for example, 2 seconds when the others are 10 seconds). What is the most likely reason?

[{'answer_text': 'Gravity suddenly disappeared for that trial.', 'text': 'Gravity suddenly disappeared for that trial.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The parachute learned to fly on its own.', 'text': 'The parachute learned to fly on its own.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The object got tired of falling slowly.', 'text': 'The object got tired of falling slowly.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The air resistance decided to stop working.', 'text': 'The air resistance decided to stop working.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The Earth spun faster during that one drop.', 'text': 'The Earth spun faster during that one drop.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'There was an error, like the parachute tangling or the timer making a mistake.', 'text': 'There was an error, like the parachute tangling or the timer making a mistake.', 'weight': 100}]
Gravity suddenly disappeared for that trial.
The parachute learned to fly on its own.
The object got tired of falling slowly.
The air resistance decided to stop working.
The Earth spun faster during that one drop.
There was an error, like the parachute tangling or the timer making a mistake.
Question 19
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
What is the main benefit of recording your drop times in a data table?

[{'answer_text': 'It keeps your information organized so you can easily compare the results.', 'text': 'It keeps your information organized so you can easily compare the results.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'It makes the parachute look more professional.', 'text': 'It makes the parachute look more professional.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It automatically fixes any mistakes you made during the test.', 'text': 'It automatically fixes any mistakes you made during the test.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It helps the parachute fall much slower.', 'text': 'It helps the parachute fall much slower.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It changes the weight of the object being dropped.', 'text': 'It changes the weight of the object being dropped.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'It stops the wind from blowing your parachute away.', 'text': 'It stops the wind from blowing your parachute away.', 'weight': 0}]
It keeps your information organized so you can easily compare the results.
It makes the parachute look more professional.
It automatically fixes any mistakes you made during the test.
It helps the parachute fall much slower.
It changes the weight of the object being dropped.
It stops the wind from blowing your parachute away.
Question 20
multiple_choice_question
multiple_choice_question
In your data table, if you subtract the average time without a parachute from the average time with a parachute, what does this answer tell you?

[{'answer_text': 'How much heavier the parachute is.', 'text': 'How much heavier the parachute is.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'How many extra seconds the parachute added to the fall time.', 'text': 'How many extra seconds the parachute added to the fall time.', 'weight': 100}, {'answer_text': 'The exact distance from the window to the ground.', 'text': 'The exact distance from the window to the ground.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'How fast the wind was blowing that day.', 'text': 'How fast the wind was blowing that day.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The final grade for your science project.', 'text': 'The final grade for your science project.', 'weight': 0}, {'answer_text': 'The number of strings used on the parachute.', 'text': 'The number of strings used on the parachute.', 'weight': 0}]
How much heavier the parachute is.
How many extra seconds the parachute added to the fall time.
The exact distance from the window to the ground.
How fast the wind was blowing that day.
The final grade for your science project.
The number of strings used on the parachute.
